# Transcrição: 5. GA- Data Limits

- **Fonte:** 5. GA- Data Limits
- **Duração:** 02:29
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:29]

So you might be wondering with the changes that Facebook are making and the iOS 14 things that have happened with data, what's happening with Google Analytics. So Google Analytics will collect all of the data that you are requesting of it. So if you've installed it through, let's say Shopify or WooCommerce or ClickFunnels or anything like that, and you've just added the script to your site, then Google Analytics itself will pick up data on where it can. The page that's visited, the elements that are clicked, how long someone spent on the site, things like that. Now, if you're driving less than 200,000 sessions per day, so if you could see session as someone visiting your site and then staying there for a period of time, if you're below 200,000 sessions a day, you're good. The report will stay fairly up to date, so when you're looking at your data, it'll be fairly accurate, et cetera, et cetera. As soon as you go over 200 sessions per day, then the reporting will only be refreshed once a day. That's on the free version of Google Analytics. They obviously have their own constraints with servers and things like that. So in that case, you really need to upgrade to Analytics 360 to allow yourself to get more hits. So for example, like 200,000 sessions per day, let's say you're paying $2 per click, that's $400,000 a day in ad spend if one session is one click. So as long as you're below that, you're kind of okay. Now, even if you're doing, let's say you're doing $50,000 a day ad spend, that's still potentially, someone might come back in the day as well, say 50,000 sessions per day, you're still good. So the solution around the FAT framework is really made for businesses that are not million dollars spend businesses a month and things like that, that the program is designed for small and medium size businesses for which the 200,000 sessions per day is generally not going to be a problem. So if you're using an ads platform, you're using Google Analytics and some sales platform like Shopify, in most cases, the data that you're able to see in Google Analytics should be fairly accurate, it should have all the data you're sending it and that should give you the insights that you need as well.
